meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-quality client projects at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: Stop following endless tutorials and start shipping. By utilizing a production-ready SaaS starter kit, you bypass boilerplate fatigue. Combine Antigravity’s architecture generation with Claude Code’s autonomous execution to build, iterate, and deploy custom SaaS applications in hours, not weeks. Focus your energy on unique business logic, not repetitive setup.
content.intro:
You know the cycle: another 10-hour course, another 'To-Do' list app, and the crushing realization that you still don't know how to architect a real-world multi-tenant SaaS. You're trapped in tutorial hell, trading your most valuable asset—time—for the illusion of progress while your client projects languish in 'development' limbo.

It’s time to stop learning and start building. Professional freelancers don't reinvent the wheel; they leverage battle-tested foundations. By integrating a modular SaaS starter kit with AI orchestration, you transform from a perpetual student into a high-velocity architect capable of shipping production-grade software before your next invoice is due.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project structure using a proven SaaS starter template to ensure authentication, billing, and database schemas are pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular SaaS architecture including Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integration. Prioritize scalability and clean separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on Antigravity architecture specs'

steps.title: Configure Multi-Tenancy
steps.description: Define your database access patterns to ensure data isolation between client organizations, a critical requirement for any B2B SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Row Level Security (RLS) policies for a multi-tenant SaaS schema in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review current database schema and apply RLS policies for tenant isolation'

steps.title: Automate API Endpoint Creation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold your CRUD operations, ensuring consistent error handling and type safety across your API layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create RESTful API endpoints for user resource management with Zod validation and comprehensive logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate API routes with Zod validation for user profiles'

steps.title: Implement Billing Webhooks
steps.description: Securely integrate Stripe webhooks to handle subscription lifecycle events, ensuring your SaaS revenue stream is robust and error-free.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Stripe webhook handler for subscription creation, upgrades, and cancellations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement stripe-webhook route and update user subscription status in DB'

steps.title: Ship and Verify
steps.description: Run your final test suite and deploy to a production environment, ensuring all environment variables are correctly mapped.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test plan for the newly built SaaS features and verify deployment readiness.

pro_tips: Always store your environment variables in a local .env.vault file to prevent accidental credential exposure.
Use Claude Code to 'refactor' your starter kit code into smaller, domain-driven modules immediately after initial generation.
Treat your starter kit as a 'living product'—maintain a private repository of your own custom components to reuse across multiple client projects.
Prioritize type-safety with TypeScript across the entire stack; it is the single biggest time-saver during the debugging phase.
Automate your feedback loop by linking your Claude Code logs directly into a Slack channel to monitor build failures in real-time.
content.next_steps: Once your core SaaS skeleton is live, begin adding unique features that differentiate your specific client's value proposition. Focus on building the 'core loop' first, then iterate based on user testing.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of profit. You have the tools, the architecture, and the strategy to dominate your freelance market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
